claims.summary: Marker stripping can destroy spatial evidence and create false empty-content results.
claims.disposition: Verified the current single-pass matcher records raw start/end spans and longest matches without cascading second-order removal.

claims.summary: Dense structural language may be suppressed when it resembles scaffolding.
claims.disposition: Mechanism correction: arbitrary structural language is not in the marker list, and dense no-marker content is explicitly tested as active.

claims.summary: Contextual and boundary occurrences should remain auditable.
claims.disposition: Verified placement and quoted occurrence counts are retained in bounded cleanup evidence.

claims.summary: Semantic-density scoring should change whether output is accepted or retained.
claims.disposition: Preserved as Tier 5 model-quality/history behavior; current evidence route changes no acceptance policy.
